---
title: "Exercise Set Title"
description: "Practice set for a single concept or tight module slice."
page-type: exercise
status: draft
update-status: major-refresh
level: foundation
topic: replace-me
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/replace-me/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Check recall and computation."
  - "Train proof or transfer skill."
  - "Point the reader to the next page."
applications:
  - replace-me
keywords:
  - exercises
  - replace-me
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: annual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: true
---

<!--
Before writing:
1. Decide the scope: one concept or one narrow cluster only
2. Make sure the problems progress from recall to transfer
3. Write solutions after checking difficulty balance
4. Update freshness fields after checking sources
-->

## Scope and Goals

State what this set is training: fluency, proof habits, computation, modeling, or transfer.

## Prerequisites

- prerequisite 1
- prerequisite 2

## Warm-Up Problems

1. problem
2. problem

## Core Problems

1. problem
2. problem
3. problem

## Proof Problems

1. problem
2. problem

## Computational or Applied Problems

1. problem
2. problem

## Hints

Keep hints separate from the problem statements.

1. hint
2. hint
3. hint

## Full Solutions

### Solution 1

Write the reasoning, not just the answer.

### Solution 2

Write the reasoning, not just the answer.

## Common Errors

- error 1
- error 2

## What To Do Next

Tell the reader what page, concept, or application to open next.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [Source title](https://example.com) - `First pass` - why it is included
- [Source title](https://example.com) - `Paper bridge` - why it is included
